user_query:
We’re onboarding five critical vendors across different industries and need a formal third‑party risk assessment plus a portfolio roll‑up. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and write all deliverables under output/ using the exact paths listed.

Read these files:
- input/vendors.json — vendor assessment data with attributes per domain
- input/portfolio_spend.csv — annual spend and operational dependency percentages
- input/previous_scores.json — last quarter’s aggregate scores for comparison
- input/company_context.json — inputs for cost‑of‑failure calculations

Using the five‑domain framework (Security Posture, Financial Stability, Compliance & Regulatory, Operational Dependency, Data Handling; each 0–100), compute domain scores strictly by these rules and clamp each domain to the 0–100 range:
- Security Posture (+20 SOC 2 Type II current; +15 pen test within 12 months; +15 incident response plan; +15 encryption at rest and in transit; +10 MFA enforced; +10 completed security questionnaire; +15 disclosed subprocessor list)
- Financial Stability (+25 growing revenue trend, +10 flat, 0 declining; +20 runway >18 months; +15 customer concentration <20%; +15 public or audited statements; +15 no material litigation; +10 acceptable credit rating)
- Compliance & Regulatory (+20 industry certifications current; +20 GDPR/CCPA compliant; +15 DPA signed; +15 clean regulatory audit history; +15 right to audit clause; +15 data residency requirements met)
- Operational Dependency (+20 SLA with financial penalties; +20 uptime >99.9% trailing 12 months; +15 DR tested annually; −20 single point of failure for our business; +15 migration plan documented; +15 API/export capability; +15 lock‑in risk assessment)
- Data Handling (+20 data classification documented; +20 retention/deletion policies; +20 breach notification <72h; +15 data portability guaranteed; AI/ML training on our data: +15 if opt‑out available, −10 if no opt‑out; +10 access logging/audit trail)

Aggregate score = sum of domain scores (0–500). Classify risk tier: Low (400–500), Moderate (300–399), High (200–299), Critical (0–199). Also include the review cadence and action per tier (Annual/Standard; Semi‑annual/Remediation required; Quarterly/Escalation; Monthly/Exit plan).

Deliverables to write:
1) Per‑vendor scorecards (kebab‑case filenames) with domain breakdowns, aggregate score, risk tier, review cadence, and 2–3 industry‑specific risk notes:
- output/scorecards/medsys-cloud.json
- output/scorecards/finguard-analytics.json
- output/scorecards/legaldocs-pro.json
- output/scorecards/factorynet-iot.json
- output/scorecards/shopscale-cdn.json
Each JSON must include: name, industry, function, security_posture, financial_stability, compliance_regulatory, operational_dependency, data_handling, aggregate_score, risk_tier, review_cadence, and a remediation_recommendations array (at least 3 items for any High or Critical vendor).

2) Portfolio summary JSON at output/portfolio_summary.json with:
- total_vendors
- tier_distribution (counts by Low/Moderate/High/Critical)
- top_concentration_risks (top 3 by dependency_percent, each: vendor, function, dependency_percent)
- annual_vendor_spend_total (sum of spend)
- high_critical_spend_total and high_critical_spend_percent (spend and % for vendors in High or Critical tiers)

3) Quarterly review report at output/quarterly_review.md that includes:
- A “Score changes since last review” section summarizing previous vs current aggregate for each vendor, and clearly flag any drop greater than 10 points
- SLA performance and uptime observations inferred from the input data
- Contract renewal timelines or watch‑items (e.g., subprocessors, audit rights) and negotiation leverage
- Alternative vendor benchmarking or contingency thoughts for any High/Critical vendors
Also write a machine‑readable delta file at output/score_changes.json listing, for each vendor: previous, current, delta, and flagged_drop_gt_10 (boolean).

4) Cost of vendor failure at output/cost_of_failure.json. Use formulas from the framework with the parameters in input/company_context.json and the following explicit rules:
- expected_downtime_days_by_tier (provided in input/company_context.json)
- Revenue loss = daily_revenue_usd × expected_downtime_days
- Recovery cost = recovery_cost_multiplier_by_tier[tier] × annual_spend_usd
- Compliance penalty = a JSON object with {min_usd, max_usd} using per‑vendor overrides if present, else default range
- Reputation damage = churn_rate × customer_ltv_usd × affected_customers_estimate[vendor]
- Operational disruption = staff_idle_cost_usd_per_day × expected_downtime_days × (dependency_percent/100)
Include a per‑vendor breakdown with all components, the vendor’s tier, and a total_estimated_cost_usd = sum of components using the midpoint of the compliance penalty range.

Use only the provided input files, and write only to the specified output/ paths.
